Thanks for the opening, but 6.45 is well above where this needs to land for us to make the economics work. We are prepared to move quickly if the structure is straightforward, and I can open at 1.88 per unit as a realistic starting point from our side. If there is flexibility tied to volume or term, we can discuss that, but we would need a dramatically lower range to continue seriously. The supplier opened far above our workable range, so the best move is to counter-anchor rather than accept or walk away. Even with a firm market backdrop, preserving downstream margin requires signaling a much lower buying position early.

A measured concession keeps the negotiation alive, signals willingness to move, and avoids ending discussions prematurely.
